title: Chatbot trip planning
description: A pleasant six-turn travel planning conversation with steadily growing context.

events.text: Plan a three-day route through the Dolomites that keeps driving under two hours per day.
Start in Bolzano, sleep near Ortisei, then loop through Val Gardena, Seceda and Lago di Braies with short hikes each afternoon.
Make it friendlier for a parent travelling with a seven-year-old.
Swap the longest hut walk for cable-car viewpoints, add playground stops and schedule the lake visit early to avoid crowds.
Now turn it into a packing and booking checklist.
Book two bases, reserve lift tickets, pack layers, rain shells, snacks, offline maps, booster seat details and a compact first-aid kit.
Add restaurant ideas that do not require long dinners.
Prioritize refugios and casual pizzerias near the route, keep one picnic dinner in the plan, and book only the final night so tired evenings stay flexible.
What if rain closes the cable cars on day two?
Move the mountain viewpoint to day three, use the Ortisei pool and wood-carving museum as the rainy-day anchor, and keep the drive short by staying in the same valley.
Finish with a text I can send to my partner.
Here is the short version: two easy bases, cable-car views instead of long climbs, early lake visit, flexible dinners, rainy-day backup in Ortisei, and a kid-friendly checklist before we book.
